class.name: FmodAudioEffectFilter
class.brief_description: A multi-mode filter effect supporting lowpass, highpass, and bandpass filter types.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectFilter is a versatile filter that can operate in multiple modes (lowpass, highpass, bandpass) to shape the frequency content of an audio signal. It provides control over cutoff frequency, rolloff steepness, gain, and resonance. This effect is useful for removing unwanted frequencies, creating muffled or telephone-like sounds, isolating frequency bands, or adding character to audio through resonance.

members.member: The cutoff frequency of the filter in Hertz (Hz). Frequencies above this point are attenuated (lowpass), below this point are attenuated (highpass), or a band around this point is passed (bandpass) depending on the filter mode.
The rolloff steepness of the filter, specified in dB per octave. Determines how sharply frequencies beyond the cutoff are attenuated. See [enum FilterDB] for available values.
The gain applied to frequencies within the passband, specified as a linear value. Values above 1.0 boost the filtered frequencies, while values below 1.0 attenuate them.

The amount of resonance (boost) applied around the cutoff frequency. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values create a more pronounced peak at the cutoff frequency, useful for creating vocal-like or synthesizer filter effects.

constants.constant: 6 dB per octave rolloff. Gentle slope, minimal effect on neighboring frequencies.
12 dB per octave rolloff. Moderate slope, good for general filtering purposes.
18 dB per octave rolloff. Steep slope, stronger frequency isolation.
24 dB per octave rolloff. Very steep slope, aggressive filtering with minimal bleed-through of unwanted frequencies.
